brief_notes: Comprehensive network analysis of the FlyWire whole-brain Drosophila connectome. Strong quantitative results: small-worldness coefficient of 86.2, giant SCC covering 93.3% of neurons, rich club of 30% of neurons. Good cross-species comparisons and neuropil-specific analysis. Methods are well-described with appropriate null models (ER, CFG). Neurotransmitter composition analysis of motifs and reciprocal connections adds biological depth. Some tables use qualitative placeholders instead of exact values for comparison species. Discussion connects findings to functional implications. Strong overall contribution to connectomics.
